Moraines, long ridges of rubble, and milky turquoise meltwater lakes glowed from the rock flour suspended in them. Despite the destructive force of the eruption, the resilience of life was evident. Bees, with their sophisticated communication systems, were already navigating the new landscape, finding new resources and avoiding danger.
